Contributes plugins for Antigravity agent. Plugins are copied to ~/.gemini/<ideName>/plugins/ when the extension is installed.
Unique identifier for the plugin. Must be unique within this extension. Will be prefixed with extension ID when stored.
Relative path to the plugin folder within the extension.
(Optional) Human-readable description of what the plugin does.

Contributes plugins for Antigravity agent. Each plugin folder is copied to `~/.gemini/<ideName>/plugins/<extensionId>.<pluginName>/` when the extension is installed.

**Expected plugin folder structure:**

```
plugins/my-plugin/
  skills/             # Agent skills
    my-skill/
      SKILL.md
  mcp_config.json     # MCP server definitions (optional)
```

When the extension is uninstalled, the plugin folder is automatically removed.

Controls whether related files should be rendered in the chat input.

Global auto approve also known as "YOLO mode" disables manual approval completely for all tools in all workspaces, allowing the agent to act fully autonomously. This is extremely dangerous and is *never* recommended, even containerized environments like Codespaces and Dev Containers have user keys forwarded into the container that could be compromised.

This feature disables critical security protections and makes it much easier for an attacker to compromise the machine.

Auto approval for '{0}' is restricted via {1}.
Auto approval for '{0}' is restricted via {1}.

Global auto approve also known as "YOLO mode" disables manual approval completely for _all tools in all workspaces_, allowing the agent to act fully autonomously. This is extremely dangerous and is *never* recommended, even containerized environments like [Codespaces](https://github.com/features/codespaces) and [Dev Containers](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers) have user keys forwarded into the container that could be compromised.

**This feature disables [critical security protections](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/security) and makes it much easier for an attacker to compromise the machine.**

One or more glob pattern (separated by comma) that describe for which files the instructions apply to. Based on these patterns, the file is automatically included in the prompt, when the context contains a file that matches one or more of these patterns.
